title: fix race conditions in start/attach logic
body:
The current code did something like this:
lock()
getState()
unlock()

if state != running
  lock()
  getState() == running -> error
  unlock()

This of course is wrong because between the first unlock() and second lock() call another process could have modified the state. This meant that sometimes you would get a weird error on start because the internal setup errored as the container was already running.

In general any state check without holding the lock is incorrect and will result in race conditions. As such refactor the code to combine both StartAndAttach and Attach() into one function that can handle both. With that we can move the running check into the locked code.

Also use typed error for this specific error case then the callers can check and ignore the specific error when needed. This also allows us to fix races in the compat API that did a similar racy state check.

Fixes #23246

comments.body:
There is one change in behaivor in the code,
Currently `podman start name` does not print the name when the container is already running. I find this rather confusing and broken as it sort of breaks the idempotence of the start command. This patch always shows the name regardless of state. I think the only place where this makes sense is when using --all so I will fix the code to handle the all case.
can it be one of these weird "Docker does it" cases?

> can it be one of these weird "Docker does it" cases?

I just tested and docker behaves like this commit does. It always prints the name.
